question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CryptoCreate transaction that results in an entity stored with deleted=null, so that findByAlias() using 'deleted <> true' (which evaluates NULL <> true as NULL/false in SQL) silently excludes the entity from alias lookups, causing downstream mirror node records referencing that alias to be missing or unresolved?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user trigger creation of an entity whose deleted column is never explicitly set (deleted=null), so that findByEvmAddress() with 'deleted <> true' excludes it while the graphql EntityRepository's 'deleted is not true' would include it, causing inconsistent entity resolution and incorrect records exported across mirror node components?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that sets an entity's alias to a byte sequence that collides with another entity's alias in the entity table, causing findByAlias() to return the wrong entity ID and leading to incorrect records being associated with the wrong account in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit an EthereumTransaction referencing an EVM address that matches a long-zero address pattern, causing findByEvmAddress() to return an incorrect entity ID (since DomainUtils.fromEvmAddress is checked first in EntityIdServiceImpl), resulting in incorrect contract records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable)] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause a large number of entity IDs (approaching MAX_CACHE_LOAD_ENTRIES=30000) to be queued for EVM address lookup via findEvmAddressesByIds(), so that the batching logic in SyntheticLogListener produces partial results, causing some synthetic contract log topics to retain stale long-zero addresses instead of real EVM addresses in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractCreate transaction whose sidecar record contains a ContractBytecode with a contract ID that maps to an existing ACCOUNT-type entity, causing updateContractType() to silently reclassify that account as CONTRACT in the entity table, resulting in incorrect entity type records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable)] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that create entities with a deleted=true status but a non-null evm_address, so that findEvmAddressesByIds() (which has no deleted filter: 'where id in (?1) and length(evm_address) > 0') returns EVM addresses for deleted entities, causing synthetic contract log topics to be updated with addresses of deleted entities in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CryptoDelete transaction to delete an account, then immediately submit a new CryptoCreate that reuses the same alias, exploiting a race condition in findByAlias() where the deleted check 'deleted <> true' may return the new entity before the old deletion is fully committed, causing duplicate or incorrect alias-to-ID mappings in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractCreate transaction with an EVM address that is exactly 20 bytes of zeros (the zero address), causing findByEvmAddress() to return an empty Optional and EntityIdServiceImpl to log a recoverable error, resulting in the contract's records being silently dropped from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a sidecar-bearing transaction with a ContractBytecode whose contractId has a null or zero entity ID, causing updateContractType() to execute 'update entity set type = CONTRACT where id in (:ids)' with id=0, potentially matching a system entity and corrupting its type in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an alias that is an empty byte array (zero-length), causing findByAlias() to execute 'where alias = ?1' with an empty byte array and potentially match entities that have an empty alias stored, returning an incorrect entity ID for mirror node record resolution?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a high volume of synthetic transfer transactions referencing many distinct entity IDs, causing findEvmAddressesByIds() to be called with an iterable exceeding the PostgreSQL IN-clause parameter limit, resulting in a query error that causes all synthetic log EVM address updates to fail and incorrect long-zero addresses to be exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByAlias() returns only the entity ID (not the full entity) so that the caller in EntityIdServiceImpl cannot verify the entity type, allowing a TOKEN or TOPIC entity to be resolved as an ACCOUNT when its alias matches, causing incorrect entity type associations in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction referencing an EVM address that is exactly 20 bytes but encodes a valid long-zero address (shard=0, realm=0, num=X), causing EntityIdServiceImpl.findByEvmAddress() to resolve it via DomainUtils.fromEvmAddress() without querying the database, bypassing the deleted check entirely and returning an entity ID for a deleted contract?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a sidecar migration record with a large batch of contract IDs (approaching IN_CLAUSE_LIMIT=32767) that includes IDs of non-contract entities, causing updateContractType() to bulk-reclassify those entities as CONTRACT without updating entity_history in the same atomic operation, leading to type inconsistency between entity and entity_history tables in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CryptoCreate with an ECDSA secp256k1 public key alias (33 bytes with proto prefix = 35 bytes), so that EntityIdServiceImpl first tries findByAlias() which returns empty (entity not yet in DB), then falls back to findByAliasEvmAddress() which derives the EVM address, causing a double-lookup that may resolve to a different entity than intended and export incorrect records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable)] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the SyntheticLogListener's Caffeine cache to be populated with a stale (pre-deletion) EVM address for an entity, so that subsequent synthetic logs for that entity use the cached stale address even after the entity is deleted, causing incorrect EVM addresses in contract log topics exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that causes the importer to call findByAlias() with a null byte array, triggering a NullPointerException or unexpected SQL behavior that causes the entire record file processing to fail, resulting in missing records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractCall transaction referencing an EVM address of incorrect length (not 20 bytes), causing findByEvmAddress() to execute a byte-array comparison against a malformed input and either return no result or match an unintended entity, leading to missing contract records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a sidecar record with duplicate contract IDs in the ContractBytecode list, causing updateContractType() to be called with duplicate IDs in the iterable, and if the underlying SQL driver deduplicates them differently than expected, result in partial type updates that leave some entity records with incorrect types in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the semantic difference between 'deleted <> true' (importer) and 'deleted is not true' (graphql) by creating an entity whose deleted field is null, so that the importer's findByAlias() misses it during entity ID resolution, causing the importer to fail to link transactions to the correct entity and export records with missing entity associations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that creates a contract with an evm_address of length 0 (empty byte array stored as non-null), causing findEvmAddressesByIds() with 'length(evm_address) > 0' to exclude it, so that synthetic contract log topics for that contract retain long-zero addresses instead of the real EVM address in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CryptoTransfer that references an account by alias, where the alias lookup via findByAlias() returns empty because the entity has deleted=null (excluded by 'deleted <> true'), causing EntityIdServiceImpl to log a recoverable error and the transfer record to be exported with an unresolved entity ID?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractUpdate transaction with a sidecar migration record that references an entity ID belonging to a TOPIC or FILE entity, causing updateContractType() to reclassify it as CONTRACT, which then causes the entity to be incorrectly exported as a contract record to mirror nodes instead of its original type?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an EVM address that matches both an existing entity's evm_address and another entity's alias (since EntityIdServiceImpl checks evm_address first), causing findByEvmAddress() to return the wrong entity ID and associate the transaction with the incorrect entity in mirror node exports?
